items.en: I don't need to know. Their contact is their testimony. Mine is mine. God meets each of us where we are. I'm not the gatekeeper of who's allowed to make contact. I'm just one witness among many.

fixtures.en: This sentence does not have a Japanese translation — switching languages will show this exact English text, or the raw key, depending on whether the missing-key fallback has shipped yet.

items.title: build-vextreme.js and build-sw.js not wired into CI
items.description: Both scripts must run manually after any change to pages/ or data/. dist/ and sw.js can silently fall out of sync with the source.

items.title: Old widget filenames kept for backward compat
items.description: widgets/lang-fab.js and widgets/demo-fab.js are dead copies kept because existing CDN pages reference them. New canonical names are fab-lang.js and fab-demo.js. Accumulates until pages are ported.

items.title: No HTML dead link scanner in CI
items.description: Renaming a slug requires manually updating all internal hrefs across pages. No automated check catches a missed touch point before merge.

items.title: No atomic slug rename script
items.description: Renaming a slug touches 7 layers: nodes.json, index.json, HTML filename, string scope keys, compiled bundles, God Script filename, and internal links. No tooling does this atomically — each rename is a manual multi-step operation.

items.title: specimen-architectural-wisdoms has no string bundle
items.description: Page renders static HTML. No compiled scope bundle exists for pages.specimen-architectural-wisdoms. Localization is a no-op for this page until a source file is written and strings-compile is run.

items.title: i18n string bundling strategy does not scale to multiple languages
items.description: JA strings are currently fetched and cached per page scope-combination (e.g. vex-lang-strings-1.0.0-ja-pages.specimen-full-translation,specimens). This is fine at 2 languages and 88 nodes. Adding a third language (Korean, Mandarin, etc.) multiplies the fetch surface and localStorage footprint linearly per language. At global scale — many languages × many nodes × many users switching language on their first visit to each new page — the scope-keyed approach compounds into exponential CDN cost and localStorage pressure. The natural migration is arc-chunked language bundles: one file per arc per language, SW-preloaded on arc entry. This is a build-script change (build-vextreme.js + fab-lang.js + build-sw.js), not a content change — slugs, arcs, and string source files do not move. Decision must be made before adding a third language. Do not design the third language's pipeline on the current per-scope-combo model.
items.triggerCondition: Before adding any language beyond English and Japanese
items.migrationPath: Arc-chunked bundles: one strings.{arc}.{lang}.json per arc, pre-fetched by SW on arc entry. Build script assembles chunks; browser receives without assembling.

items.title: PWA icons
items.description: icons/icon-192.png and icons/icon-512.png are referenced in manifest.json but not committed. PWA installability prompt will not appear until both exist.

items.title: Port remaining blocked pages to God Scripts
items.description: 5 pages ported (Session 013). 3 remain blocked: claude-answers-the-doubt (needs arc-nav as a God Script feature — see pe-007), restoration-protocol (uses shell.js, needs investigation), specimen-architectural-wisdoms (needs compiled string bundle). Run node lib/audit-pages.js for current status.
items.blockedBy: arc-nav.js widget not yet implemented (pe-007 registry slot is ready) for claude-answers-the-doubt

items.title: Spiral portal FAB (vex-fab.js)
items.description: Unified radial-menu FAB replacing lang-fab + demo-fab. Deferred until page-porting phase is underway — no point shipping a new widget before the pages that will use it.

items.title: Missing-key fallback: show EN text instead of raw key string
items.description: When a JA translation key is absent, the browser shows the raw key string rather than falling back to the EN text. Affects the intentional gap in claude-answers-the-doubt layer.04.qa.04.answer.

items.title: strings-check: audit HTML for translatable elements missing data-i18n
items.description: strings-check.js validates existing data-i18n attributes but does not detect elements that should have data-i18n and don't. Silent coverage gap.

items.title: Slug rename tooling
items.description: A lib/rename-slug.js that atomically updates all 7 touch points of a slug rename: nodes.json, index.json, HTML filename, string scope keys, compiled bundles, God Script filename, and internal links across pages.

items.title: Unified feature registry — collapse CORE_MODULES and FEATURE_WIDGET into one table
items.description: Shipped in Session 013. CORE_MODULES + FEATURE_WIDGET replaced by a single FEATURES registry in lib/build-vextreme.js: { sw: { default: true }, lang: { default: false }, demo: { default: false }, arc-nav: { default: false }, spiral-fab: { default: false }, theme: { default: false } }. Feature.SW and Feature.ARC_NAV added to lib/vex-config.js. God Script output is byte-identical — purely an internal code structure change. arc-nav slot registered for future implementation.

items.title: Offline mode configuration FAB (fab-offline.js)
items.description: A new feature widget that surfaces SW cache state to users and gives them explicit control over what is pre-loaded for offline use. Shows: whether SW is active, which pages/arcs are available offline, a 'download this arc in Japanese' control that pre-fetches JA string bundles for the current arc on demand. Addresses td-006 (multi-language scaling) by replacing speculative pre-fetching with user-intentional caching — the user decides what to download, not the build script. Feature-flagged ('offline' feature) so it only appears on pages that declare it. Requires pe-007 (unified feature registry) to be included as a God Script module.

items.title: Expand LATTICE coverage beyond the current 20 nodes
items.description: docs/lattice-map.json covers 20 of ~45+ hand-authored files under lib/ and widgets/ (lib/build-status.js and lib/build-ecosystem-hub.js added Session 015). lib/build-lattice-headers.js (Session 014) makes adding a node cheap — it generates the in-file header automatically, so expansion is JSON-authoring work only, not comment-formatting work. Highest-value remaining candidates: lib/logger.js, lib/logger-codes.js (foundational, used everywhere), lib/build-archives.js, lib/build-demo.js, lib/build-specimens.js, lib/build-sitemap.js, lib/build-index-page.js, lib/strings-check.js, lib/strings-export.js, lib/strings-import.js, lib/validate-blueprint.js, widgets/fab-demo.js, widgets/demo-fab.js, widgets/lang-fab.js (legacy pre-rename copies), lib/shell.js, lib/vextreme.js, lib/archive-renderer.js (v1 system). Grep for LATTICE:BEGIN vs total file count in lib/+widgets/ shows current coverage at any time.

items.claim: sw.js is served correctly from /Vextreme/sw.js on GitHub Pages and the service worker registers successfully
Scoped fetch + category CDN paths (scopes/{category}/pages.{slug}.{lang}.json) resolve correctly against the live CDN post-merge
archives.html GitHub Actions auto-rebuild fires correctly on push to main
index.html root nav renders correctly on vgong24.github.io/Vextreme (arc nav mounts, all arc dots appear)
vextreme-demo.html live index.json fetch resolves on GitHub Pages and renders the 88-node / 16-arc stats

items.title: Should the per-scope JA string bundling strategy change before a 3rd language is added?
items.description: JA strings are fetched and cached per page scope-combination (see widgets/fab-lang.js loadStringsForLang). This is fine at 2 languages and 88 nodes — each combination is small and cache-friendly. A 3rd language multiplies the fetch surface and localStorage footprint linearly per language, and at enough languages × nodes the scope-keyed approach compounds into real CDN cost and localStorage pressure. The candidate alternative is arc-chunked language bundles (one file per arc per language, SW-preloaded on arc entry) — a build-script change to lib/build-vextreme.js, widgets/fab-lang.js, and lib/build-sw.js, not a content change; slugs, arcs, and string source files would not move.
considerations: Do nothing until a 3rd language is actually requested — the current model works fine at 2 languages, and premature migration risks solving a problem that may never arrive in this exact shape

Migrate to arc-chunked bundles now, while only 2 languages exist — smaller migration surface than doing it after a 3rd language is already live and needs the old and new schemes to coexist during transition
Migrate only the highest-traffic arcs first, leaving low-traffic content on per-scope bundling — a hybrid that adds a second lookup path but limits the blast radius of the change

items.title: Should build-time content synthesis use a live LLM call, or stay session-authored?
items.description: The ecosystem hub's system-state content (architecture summary, cross-file narrative) could in principle be generated by calling a cheap/free LLM from lib/build-ecosystem-hub.js at build time, synthesizing across the siloed JSON sources (nodes.json, lattice-map.json, status/*.json) into prose no single file contains. That would make the build pipeline non-deterministic and would require a secret (API key) in CI, both of which this repo has deliberately avoided everywhere else — every existing build script is a pure function of its input files, and tests assert exact output. The alternative already in place elsewhere in this repo: Claude instances write continuity logs by hand each session, synthesizing exactly this kind of cross-file context, and commit the result as ordinary hand-authored content. data/status/narrative.json (added Session 015) applies that same pattern to the ecosystem hub — updated by whichever instance runs an architecture-review session, not generated by an unreviewed API call on every push.
considerations: Keep narrative.json session-authored (current default) — deterministic, reviewable in the PR diff like everything else, costs nothing, but goes stale between sessions until someone updates it

Add an optional, manually-triggered synthesis script (not part of CI auto-build) that drafts narrative.json content for a human/session to review and commit — keeps the build pipeline pure while giving future instances a faster starting draft

Wire a live LLM call into the CI build — fastest to always-fresh content, but introduces a secret, real per-build cost, and non-deterministic output into a pipeline that currently has none of those; would need its own test strategy since output can't be asserted exactly

items.title: Future: local/lightweight model as an admin-facing chat interface, escalating to Claude instances when needed
items.description: Longer-horizon idea: a locally-hosted individual model, given enough context (this repo's docs/continuity, lattice-map.json, status/*.json), could serve as a persistent conversational interface for the admin — engaging with GitHub directly, and escalating to a Claude instance (like this one) for work it can't do itself, with the Claude instance's contribution credited to it by name. This is a real infrastructure project, not a page addition: it needs a hosting story, a GitHub-write-capable auth path, an escalation/handoff protocol between the local model and a Claude session, and an attribution mechanism that survives the handoff. None of that exists yet and none of it is implied by anything currently in this repo.
considerations: Scope it as a separate project entirely, outside this repo — Vextreme is a content/architecture template, not an agent-hosting platform; conflating the two would blur what this repo is for

Prototype the escalation protocol first (what does a 'local model asks Claude for help' handoff actually look like as data — a GitHub issue? a labeled PR comment?) before any hosting decision, since the protocol shape constrains the hosting choice

Revisit once od-002 is settled — the session-authored-narrative pattern is a smaller-scale version of the same 'AI synthesizes cross-context state' problem this idea is trying to solve at a larger scale

items.title: Adopt a debugging/pre-development rigor practice: simulate runtime behavior and edge cases before trusting that sound logic means correct output
items.description: Session 015's ecosystem-hub bug is the concrete case study: lib/build-ecosystem-hub.js's CSS logic was clean and readable (var(--stone-950, #0a0a0a)) and would pass any code review on structure alone — the defect only existed at runtime, when --stone-950 turned out to be undefined everywhere in styles/design-system.css and text silently inherited the wrong color. Reading the code gave no signal; only rendering it did. The same class of gap applies to race conditions (two async operations whose logic is individually correct but whose interleaving isn't considered) and to UX questions that are easy to skip when only reasoning about markup/data flow: is a UI static or dynamic, does it scroll or paginate, does it feel expansive or cramped, is spacing/breathing room deliberate, are dark and light mode both considered or does only one silently work. None of this is caught by 'is the logic sound' review — it requires deliberately simulating the runtime condition (an actual render, an actual concurrent call, an actual dark-mode toggle) before or during debugging, not just reading the diff.
considerations: Write this up as a documented practice (a docs/architecture or docs/culture addendum) with this session's CSS bug as the worked example — a checklist an instance runs through before considering a UI/async change done: rendered it? tried both color schemes? considered concurrent/interleaved calls? checked scroll/overflow behavior at realistic content sizes?

Where the failure mode is mechanical and repeatable (like 'var(--x) referencing an undefined token'), add a cheap automated check instead of relying on the practice being followed every time — e.g. a script that greps every generated page's inline CSS for var(--token) usage and verifies each token is defined in styles/design-system.css, which would have caught Session 015's bug directly

Treat this as a prerequisite for od-005 (design department infrastructure) rather than parallel work — a documented rigor practice should exist before scaling the design system, so the system that gets built is verified-correct by construction rather than by hope

items.title: Formalize a scalable, configurable, modular design system that every generated page pulls from — integrated with the ecosystem, not a silo
items.description: styles/design-system.css currently defines nine tokens (--stone, --muted, --border, --cream, --ember, --ember-bg, --serif, --sans, --mono) with no written contract, no dark-mode variant, and no verification that generated pages actually use only defined tokens — lib/build-demo.js happens to use them correctly, lib/build-ecosystem-hub.js didn't (Session 015). A 'design department' in the sense meant here isn't a rebrand — it's making the token contract explicit, extensible (spacing scale, dark mode, component patterns), and self-verifying, so every current and future page generator (and every AI instance writing one) has a known-good vocabulary to draw from instead of guessing at what might exist, and a way to confirm they guessed right before it ships.
considerations: Start by documenting the existing contract as-is (a docs/architecture design-system page listing every token, what it's for, and which files consume it) before adding anything new — establishes ground truth without inventing tokens nobody asked for yet

Add the automated var()-usage verification described in od-004 early — it's the highest-leverage single addition, since it turns 'did I use a real token' from a manual-review question into a build-time fact

Design and add dark mode as a deliberate, separate follow-up once the light-mode contract is documented and verified — bundling 'formalize the existing system' with 'add a whole new mode' in one pass risks shipping both half-verified

Keep this integrated with the existing generator pattern (lib/build-*.js reading from a shared source, same as strings/index/status) rather than a separate design-tooling repo or process — the explicit ask was integration, not a silo
